user_query:
I’m organizing our combined yoga + massage packages and need our docs and pricing to match what’s in our config and pricing rules. Please use the existing files in this workspace and produce the following:

Inputs to use:
- config/packages.yaml (canonical package definitions and add-on pricing)
- scripts/pricing.py (the tax rate and per-package bundle discounts are defined here—inspect this code to apply the correct values)
- docs/offerings.md (contains the current public list of packages; the “Current Packages” section is out of date)
- data/bookings.csv (historical bookings with package_id; use this to compute counts)

Required outputs and updates:
1) Write a consolidated JSON catalog at out/packages_catalog.json. Include one object per package defined in config/packages.yaml with the following fields:
   - package_id (from YAML)
   - name (from YAML)
   - active (bool from YAML)
   - yoga_sessions, yoga_minutes (from YAML)
   - massage_sessions, massage_minutes (from YAML)
   - included_add_ons (list from YAML)
   - base_price (from YAML)
   - discount_pct (from scripts/pricing.py; 0.0 if not specified for a package_id)
   - tax_rate (from scripts/pricing.py)
   - subtotal_before_discount (base_price + sum of included add-on costs using add_ons_pricing in YAML)
   - total_after_discount (subtotal_before_discount * (1 - discount_pct))
   - final_price_with_tax (total_after_discount * (1 + tax_rate), rounded to 2 decimals)
   - booking_count (count of rows in data/bookings.csv for the package_id)
   Use the exact calculation logic above, deriving the tax rate and any per-package discounts by inspecting scripts/pricing.py. Do not assume or hard-code different rates.

2) Before modifying any docs, compare the set of package names listed in the “## Current Packages” section of docs/offerings.md (case-insensitive) with the set of package names from config/packages.yaml. Write out/discrepancies.csv with columns: type,package_name, where type is either "config_only" (in YAML but not in the current doc section) or "doc_only" (in doc but not in YAML). This comparison should reflect the document as it exists prior to your edits.

3) Edit docs/offerings.md in place. Replace only the content of the “## Current Packages” section with an updated list that reflects the currently active packages (active: true) from config/packages.yaml. For each active package, include:
   - The package name and its tax-inclusive price (final_price_with_tax from step 1, rounded to 2 decimals)
   - A short description stating: “Includes X yoga sessions (Y min) and Z massage session(s) (W min)”
   - If there are included add-ons, list them in parentheses (e.g., “includes aromatherapy”)
   Keep all other sections and content in the document unchanged.

Notes and constraints:
- Use add-on pricing only from config/packages.yaml and the tax/discount values only from scripts/pricing.py; inspect those files to determine the correct numbers.
- Compute booking_count from data/bookings.csv by package_id.
- Ensure out/packages_catalog.json is valid JSON and out/discrepancies.csv is a valid CSV with the specified columns.
- Explicit output paths: out/packages_catalog.json, out/discrepancies.csv; modified file: docs/offerings.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
